name: Adagio Medical Holdings, Inc.
business: Adagio Medical Holdings, Inc. is a medical device company focused on developing and commercializing products for the treatment of cardiac arrhythmias with its novel, proprietary, catheter-based Ultra-Low Temperature Ablation (ULTA) technology. It sells its products directly to hospitals and medical centers for use by cardiac electrophysiologists in treating ventricular tachycardia (VT).

exposures.sentence: We are a medical device company focused on developing and commercializing products for the treatment of cardiac arrhythmias with our novel, proprietary, catheter-based Ultra-Low Temperature Ablation ( ULTA ) technology, formerly known as Ultra-Low Temperature Cryoablation ( ULTC ) technology.
We are also currently developing a next-generation ULTA technology for VT.
We have also developed a technology that utilizes ULTA in combination with PFA, which we call Pulsed Field Cryoablation ( PFCA ).

catalysts.event: Share six-month results of FULCRUM-VT trial at Heart Rhythm Society 2026 Conference
catalysts.sentence: We plan to share the six-month results of the FULCRUM-VT trial in April 2026 at the Heart Rhythm Society 2026 Conference

catalysts.event: Submit results to support application for FDA approval of vCLAS Cryoablation System
catalysts.sentence: to submit the results of this trial to support our application for FDA approval of our vCLAS TM Cryoablation System in the first half of 2026.

tamClaims.claim: The total addressable global market for VT ablation is $5.8 billion, comprising 1.6 million patients.
tamClaims.sentence: It is estimated that the total addressable global market for VT ablation is $5.8 billion, which is comprised of approximately 1.6 million patients with VT who are eligible for ablation each year.

tamClaims.claim: The U.S. addressable market for VT is $1.6 billion, comprising 200,000 patients.
tamClaims.sentence: Within the U.S., the total addressable market for VT is estimated to be $1.6 billion, comprised of 200,000 patients.

tamClaims.claim: The global electrophysiology device market was approximately $14 billion in 2025.
tamClaims.sentence: The total value of the global market for electrophysiology ( EP ) devices in 2025 was approximately $14 billion

tamClaims.claim: The EP device market is projected to grow at a CAGR of about 13% to over $33 billion by 2033.
tamClaims.sentence: According to industry sources, longer-term, the market is projected to grow at a compound annual growth rate of approximately 13%, reaching over $33 billion by 2033.

tamClaims.claim: VT ablation procedures have been growing by approximately 7% annually.
tamClaims.sentence: VT ablation procedures have been growing by approximately 7% annually
